name: T1 Glass-Ceiling Push 2026-05-13 — slot A/B/C single-batch pre-registration
operator: Claude Opus 4.7 (pd-imu-100x-researcher skill, T1 Glass-Ceiling Push mode)
user_directive: use codex cli and gemini cli. deep dive into everything you've learned in this project as a 100x researcher. BREAK T1 CCC GLASS CEILING THROUGH SYSTEMATIC DEBUGGING AND ALGO WORK.

honest_in_cohort_ceiling.name: V2 + V3-GSP nested 2-source CV
honest_in_cohort_ceiling.verdict_2026-05-12: codex brutal: in-cohort ceiling structurally closed at N=92

family_members: slot_B: V3-GSP feature substitution at item-12 chain step
slot_C: sklearn GB + K=250 at item-12 chain step
comparison_baselines_per_slot: iter12-honest on the same cohort N as slot result (N=92 after auxiliary filter)
iter34 hybrid on the same cohort N=92 (incumbent gate)
fwer_policy.screen_promotion_rule: 5-fold mean Δ ≥ +0.020 with seed std < 0.020 OR Δ ≥ +0.030 with seed std < 0.030 → promote to LOOCV; otherwise close slot without LOOCV (avoid selection leakage)
fwer_policy.joint_promotion_gate: BOTH paired bootstrap frac>0 ≥ 0.9875 AND sign-flip permutation p ≤ 0.0125 vs iter34 on N=92

cohort.filter_rules: iter34 valid-auxiliary cohort (drop NLS036 + any subject with item-15 or item-18 out of range)

A.name: 7-item-no-13 chain (architectural pruning) + CCC-descending chain order
A.hypothesis: Combining (a) removal of item 13 (CCC=0.067, IMU-noise) from iter34's 8-item chain and (b) FIXED CCC-descending chain order [12, 10, 14, 9, 11, 15, 18] (load-bearing item first) reduces gradient pollution AND provides high-confidence anchor for downstream weak items, yielding T1 sum CCC ≥ 0.7370 (Δ=+0.020) at N=92.
A.mechanism_synthesis_post_tri_cli_2026-05: Codex+Gemini converged on prior 0.03-0.12 for 7-item-drop alone; Gemini surfaced CCC-descending ordering as mechanism enhancement (prior 0.12 alone). Synthesis combines both: drop-13 removes distractor (Phase 0 +0.003 base effect) + CCC-descending provides anchor (item 12 first → conditioning for weak items). Acknowledged meta-leakage caveat: slot design motivated by Phase 0 ablation on same N=92 cohort (not pure tune-on-test — different architecture — but credibility diminished if it passes).
A.feature_pool: V2 K=500 (LGB-importance against T1 residual) — same as iter34, but residual = T1 = sum(items 9-14) computed over the same N=92 cohort
A.base_learners: LGB + XGB-hist + ExtraTrees ensemble — same as iter34
A.chain_order_rationale: Descending iter34 per-item CCC: 12=0.566, 10=0.443, 14=0.317, 9=0.234, 11=0.232, 15+18 aux at end. Item 12 anchor first → weak items condition on high-confidence anchor.
A.item13_handling: drop from chain entirely; T1 sum = sum(predicted_items_9_10_11_12_14) + train_fold_mean(item_13)
A.screen_target: Δ ≥ +0.020 5-fold OOF vs iter34 hybrid on N=92
A.loocv_target: Δ ≥ +0.025 LOOCV vs iter34 hybrid on N=92, frac>0 ≥ 0.9875
A.kill_criterion: screen Δ̄ < +0.010 OR std ≥ 0.025 → close without LOOCV

B.name: V3-GSP feature substitution at item-12 chain step
B.hypothesis: Replacing V2 K=500 features with V3-GSP low-mode-energy + en-bloc-index features (~50 features, no K=500 selection) at the item-12 step ONLY of iter34's 8-item chain raises item-12 LOOCV CCC from 0.566 to ≥ 0.62, lifting T1 sum CCC ≥ 0.7370 (Δ=+0.020) at N=92.
B.mechanism: Per codex 2026-05-12: 'feature changes the base learner predictions BEFORE stacking.' V3-GSP graph-spectrum projections are mathematically orthogonal to V2 (V2 cannot span global multi-sensor coordination). Item 12 has CCC=+0.22 of residual predictability from V3-GSP low-mode features. Targeted substitution at the load-bearing item bypasses K=500 absorption (the WALL is the selection rule, not the features).

feature_pool_per_step.item_12: V3-GSP low-mode features (drop K=500 selection; use all ~50 GSP-low-mode features)

B.base_learners: LGB + XGB-hist + ExtraTrees ensemble — same as iter34
B.screen_target: Δ ≥ +0.020 5-fold OOF on item-12 sum and T1 sum vs iter34 hybrid on N=92
B.loocv_target: Δ ≥ +0.025 LOOCV vs iter34 hybrid on N=92, frac>0 ≥ 0.9875
B.kill_criterion: screen Δ̄ < +0.010 OR std ≥ 0.025 → close without LOOCV

C.name: sklearn GradientBoostingRegressor + K=250 at item-12 chain step
C.hypothesis: Replacing the item-12 step base learner (LGB+XGB+ET ensemble) with sklearn GradientBoostingRegressor(n_estimators=300, max_depth=4, learning_rate=0.05, subsample=0.8) at K=250 univariate-corr-K-best yields item-12 LOOCV CCC ≥ 0.62, lifting T1 sum CCC ≥ 0.7370 at N=92.
C.mechanism: T3 K-sweep wall #69 (2026-05-13) revealed sklearn GB at K=250 univariate-corr-K-best has different bias-variance from LGB at low N: T3 CCC=0.4516 vs iter47 canonical LGB=0.3784 (Δ=+0.073). The hump structure peaks at K=250 with low seed std (0.008). Hypothesis: same hump curve applies to T1 item-12 residual fit. Targeted to load-bearing item only to avoid burning the lift on noise items.

feature_pool_per_step.items_9_10_11_13_14_15_18: V2 K=500 LGB-importance, base learner ensemble (iter34 canonical)
feature_pool_per_step.item_12: V2 K=250 univariate-corr-K-best (vs T1-residual), base learner = sklearn GradientBoostingRegressor

C.screen_target: Δ ≥ +0.020 5-fold OOF vs iter34 hybrid on N=92
C.loocv_target: Δ ≥ +0.025 LOOCV vs iter34 hybrid on N=92, frac>0 ≥ 0.9875
C.expected_effect: +0.005 to +0.015 (sub-MCID likely; orthogonal mechanism transfer from T3 risky)
C.kill_criterion: screen Δ̄ < +0.010 OR std ≥ 0.025 → close without LOOCV

diversity_assertion: Three mechanisms span distinct axes: A=architectural pruning (no feature change), B=feature family substitution (V3-GSP vs V2), C=model class + selection rule (sklearn GB + K=250 vs LGB+XGB+ET + K=500). B and C both target item-12 (the load-bearing item per Phase 0 ablation) by design, but use orthogonal mechanisms.

stopping_rules.hard_cap: 3 slots, no retries, no 'one more variant'
stopping_rules.soft_stop: if slot A clears Bonferroni n=4 (frac>0 ≥ 0.9875 vs BOTH baselines) by wide margin (≥0.99 against both) → stop before B/C
stopping_rules.FWER_trap_acknowledged: If slot A passes loosely (≥0.9875 only) but B/C fail, joint family-wise effective gate may be unmet — slot A would be reported as 'candidate, uncorrected pass not FWER-corrected'.

audit_trail.honest_prior_on_pass_probability: <10% per slot per skill's 2026-05-12 closure verdict (codex+kimi+deepseek+gemini consensus). Slot is justified by user override; outcome is documented either as falsification (likely) or candidate (low prior).

outcome_2026-05-13.fwer_family_status: all 3 slots closed without lockbox, family-wise gate moot
outcome_2026-05-13.tri_cli_priors_calibration: codex 0.12 (slot A) ≈ actual ~0; gemini 0.03 ≈ actual ~0. Both correctly forecast structural N=92 wall.
outcome_2026-05-13.closing_narrative: Three orthogonal mechanisms (architectural pruning, feature-family substitution, model-class+selection-rule substitution) all empirically validate codex's 2026-05-12 brutal verdict: in-cohort T1 ceiling at N=92 is structurally closed at ~0.7170-0.7285. External cohort access (PPMI/Verily, WATCH-PD, CNS Portugal, etc.) remains the only structural enabler for breaking the +0.025 MCID gate.
publishable_artifacts: T1 conformal abstention lockbox (deployment-mode secondary): 70% coverage CCC=0.7777, 50% coverage CCC=0.8338 — different estimand, FROM 2026-05-12 campaign
iter34 hygiene-corrected candidate CCC=0.7170 (N=92) — strongest in-cohort candidate, single post-publication replication target
iter12-honest canonical floor CCC=0.6550 (N=94) — defensible paper baseline
